title: potentilla egedii subsp. pacifica
intro: Potentilla egedii subsp. pacifica, also known as the Pacific cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial herb with leaves that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Pacific cinquefoil has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used topically as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
